CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

Off Calendar Jun-12-2017 Continued To Dec-05-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-18-2017 Off Calendar

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-18-2017 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jun-27-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-24-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Linda Ross of the law offices of Linda Ross (415.563.2400) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Deputy City Attorney, Brian Paul Ceballo of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (415.554.3911) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. The City has maintained its initial summary judgment burden of production. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and special interrogatory responses are factually devoid and show that plaintiff has no proof that the City created the pothole or had actual or constructive notice of the pothole. (Plaintiff's Depo., 136:15-137:11; Plaintiff's special interrogatory responses 32-40 [actual notice] and 41-48 [constructive notice].) The City also demonstrates that nobody ever complained about the pothole to the City. (Kyi and Leong Decs.) In opposition, plaintiff creates a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the City had constructive notice of the defect. (See Gov't Code sec. 835.2(b).) Plaintiff's evidence indicates that the pothole existed since 2007, which was seven years prior to the accident. (Scott Dec., par. 2.) The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)
